findings.message: Irreversible destructive action with no confirmation: clicking Delete immediately issues a DELETE request and reloads the page. A misclick deletes the item with no chance to recover.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation step before performing the delete (e.g., window.confirm(...) or a confirm modal), and/or provide an undo affordance after deletion.

findings.message: No feedback on action: the button is not disabled while the DELETE request is in flight, and there is no loading/pending indicator. Users may click repeatedly or be unsure whether the action is happening.
findings.suggestion: Track a pending state (e.g., useState) and disable the button while the request is in flight; show a spinner or 'Deleting…' label.

findings.message: No success/error feedback for a mutating action: failures from fetch are silently swallowed and success is only implied by a full page reload. Users get no toast/message confirming the deletion or surfacing an error.
findings.suggestion: Handle the fetch response (check response.ok), show an inline error message or toast on failure, and a brief success confirmation on success instead of relying on window.location.reload().

findings.message: Confusing flow: using window.location.reload() as the post-delete UX is a heavy, disorienting full-page refresh that loses scroll/focus context. Prefer updating local state in place.
findings.suggestion: Lift the items list state up (or use a data layer like React Query / SWR) and remove the deleted item from state on success instead of reloading the page.
